input.prompt: Your team is upgrading the codebase from .NET 9 to .NET 10. You have been asked to benchmark AverageWordLength, WordLengthHistogram, and UniqueWordCount to check how the upgrade will impact their performance. The source code is in the TextAnalyzer subdirectory (TextAnalyzer.cs and TextAnalyzer.csproj). We don't need anything too comprehensive, don't spend more than 10 minutes on this. Don't worry about cleaning up any files or projects you create along the way. Note: this machine may be under load from other processes. If the .NET 9 runtime is missing, install it using the official dotnet-install script from https://dot.net/v1/dotnet-install.sh (or dotnet-install.ps1 on Windows).

data.content: Both runtimes are available. I'll create a BenchmarkDotNet project that multi-targets net9.0 and net10.0.
data.reasoningText: Both .NET 9 and .NET 10 runtimes are available. I'll create a BenchmarkDotNet project that targets both `net9.0` and `net10.0` and benchmarks the three methods. Let me set this up.

+  <PropertyGroup>
+    <TargetFrameworks>net9.0;net10.0</TargetFrameworks>
+    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
+    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
+    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>

+    <PackageReference Include="BenchmarkDotNet" Version="0.14.*" />
+    <Compile Include="../TextAnalyzer/TextAnalyzer.cs" Link="TextAnalyzer.cs" />

+</Project>
+

data.content: BDN doesn't have `Core100` yet. I'll use `--runtimes` CLI flag instead.
data.reasoningText:
BenchmarkDotNet might not have `CoreRuntime.Core100` yet. Let me check available options or use a different approach. I can use the `[SimpleJob]` attribute with runtime monikers, or just let BDN auto-detect from the multi-target frameworks.

Actually, the simplest approach is to just use `BenchmarkSwitcher` or just run with the default config which auto-detects the target frameworks. Let me update the Program.cs to not specify runtimes explicitly and instead rely on the multi-targeting.
